name: The villages of Lower Aragon - circular from Monroyo
name_original: Los pueblos de la Baja Aragón - circular desde Monroyo
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Monroyo are found in the Matarraña region of Teruel, Spain, characterized by its mountainous terrain and an altitude of 857 meters. The area features ranges such as Molinera and Sabinosos, interspersed with deep ravines and dense forests of pines and holm oaks. This landscape provides a tranquil environment with minimal traffic, offering varied terrain for road cyclists. Routes often include significant ascents and can lead to summits exceeding 1,000 meters in elevation. Best road cycling routes around Monroyo The most popular road cycling route is Torre Miró Loop from Monroyo, a 29.0 miles (46.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 30 minutes to complete. This moderate route features an elevation gain of over 870 meters. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is The villages of Lower Aragon - circular from Monroyo, a difficult 52.6 miles (84.6 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain, including significant ascents and descents through the region's villages. Local road cyclists also love the Fuentespalda – Valderrobres Stone Bridge loop from Monroyo / Montroig, a 36.6 miles (59.0 km) trail leading through the Matarraña landscape, often completed in about 3 hours 5 minutes. Road cycling around Monroyo is defined by mountainous terrain, deep ravines, and dense forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging routes with substantial elevation gain. The routes in Monroyo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 180 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Monroyo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Monroyo?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes around Monroyo, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes are split evenly between moderate and difficult, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Monroyo?
A: Road cycling in Monroyo features a varied and often challenging terrain. You'll encounter mountainous landscapes with significant ascents, deep ravines, and dense forests of pines and holm oaks. The region is known for its quiet roads and minimal traffic, making it a tranquil environment for cycling.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, Monroyo is ideal for cyclists seeking a challenge. Many routes include substantial elevation gain, with some leading to summits exceeding 1,000 meters. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958904961/the-villages-of-lower-aragon-circular-from-monroyo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The villages of Lower Aragon - circular from Monroyo&lt;/a&gt; route covers 84.6 km with over 1,370 meters of ascent, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentespalda – Valderrobres Stone Bridge loop from Monroyo / Montroig&lt;/a&gt; offers over 1,050 meters of climbing.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Monroyo?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Monroyo are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959118284/torre-miro-loop-from-monroyo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre Miró Loop from Monroyo&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 46.7 km route with over 870 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes in Monroyo?
A: The region boasts several natural attractions. You can cycle past the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4363491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de Pena&lt;/a&gt; reservoir, or explore areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2882512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portellada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, known for its eroded rock formations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5292931" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas del Masmut Trail&lt;/a&gt; also offers breathtaking geological views, though some sections may be challenging for cycling.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, you can discover historical sites. The village of Monroyo itself has medieval heritage, including the 13th to 15th-century Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción and remnants of its historic castle. You might also pass through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Town of Ráfales&lt;/a&gt; on some routes, or see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of la Balma&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Monroyo?
A: The region's mountainous climate makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. While summer can be warm, the higher altitude of Monroyo (857 meters) can provide some relief compared to lower areas. Winter cycling is possible but may require more preparation due to cooler temperatures.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Monroyo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the tranquil environment, minimal traffic, and the stunning natural scenery of deep ravines and dense forests. The varied terrain, from moderate to challenging ascents, is also a significant draw.

Q: Are there services for cyclists in Monroyo?
A: Yes, accommodations in the Monroyo area often cater specifically to cyclists. You can find services such as bicycle transport, luggage transfer for stage routes, professional repair services, bike rentals, and facilities for washing and storing bikes, making it a very cyclist-friendly destination.

Q: Can I find moderate road cycling routes suitable for good fitness levels?
A: Absolutely. While there are many challenging routes, Monroyo also offers several moderate options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Calatravos – Castelserás loop from Belmonte de San José / Bellmunt de Mesquí&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 58.9 km route with around 690 meters of elevation gain, providing a rewarding ride without extreme difficulty.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by rivers or water features?
A: Yes, the region is traversed by watercourses such as the Tastavins and Monroyo rivers. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2890060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge Over the Tastavins River&lt;/a&gt; on some routes, adding to the picturesque scenery and offering pleasant views during your ride.
tips: Fuentespalda is located halfway along the road that goes from Vall-de-roures to Pena-roja and Mont-roig de Tastavins and its name would come from focem (de) spathula, the gorge of the small swordfish.
The sanctuary of Montserrat de Fórnols de Matarranya is a set of buildings made up of a church, a cloister and a well. The church is also called the hermitage of Santa Mònica.

Typical town in the interior of Teruel where peace and tranquility reign, so much so that at times you will only hear the noise of the wind or of some animal prowling around. Its little streets are very picturesque as are the views from the perimeter of the town.
